title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries, 2024

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

The map displays the geographic distribution of countries eligible for Gavi support in 2024, using a binary color scheme to distinguish between nations that qualify for financial and expert assistance and those that do not. Eligibility is determined by a Gross National Income per capita threshold of less than US$1,810 over the preceding three years. The teal shading identifies the eligible nations, which form a contiguous band across the majority of the African continent, extending from the Sahel region down through Southern Africa. This pattern includes countries such as Nigeria, Ethiopia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Kenya. Outside of Africa, the eligible category encompasses a cluster of nations in South Asia, specifically India, Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Bangladesh, as well as Myanmar and Nepal. In the Middle East, Yemen is marked as eligible, while surrounding nations remain unshaded. The distribution also includes Papua New Guinea in the Pacific and several countries in Central America and the Caribbean, such as Haiti, Honduras, and Guatemala. Conversely, the unshaded beige areas indicate countries not eligible for support, covering the entirety of North America, South America, Europe, Australia, and large portions of the Middle East and East Asia, including China and Russia. The visual data shows a clear concentration of supported nations in low-income regions of the Global South, with the highest density of eligible countries located in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia.
